title: Triaspis mooreana
intro: Triaspis mooreana, also known as Triaspis, is a perennial plant belonging to the family Apocynaceae, native to tropical regions of Africa. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with traditional uses including the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal issues, attributed to its potential health benefits of reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing. The bioactive constituents of Triaspis mooreana include alkaloids, phenolic compounds, and flavonoids, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Triaspis is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, as well as being used in the form of a poultice or salve to treat various health conditions.
